differences.description: Document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II".
Document version/status changed from "Version 20250508: This document is in Ratified state." to "Version 20260120: Official Release".
The contributors paragraph now ends with a single period after "Reinoud Zandijk" instead of the double period present in the first document.
Added a new current preface for Version 20260120 stating that all listed ISA modules have been ratified.
Added Svrsw60t59b Extension version 1.0 to the current ISA module list.
Current module list changed from three columns (Module, Version, Status) with each status shown as Ratified to two columns (Module, Version) after stating all modules are ratified.
Module names in the current module list were normalized: "Smctr" became "Smctr Extension", "Svvptc" became "Svvptc Extension", and "Pointer Masking" became "Pointer-Masking Extensions".
Added a change log entry for changes since 20250508: "Addition of the Svrsw60t59b extension for additional PTE reserved-for-software bits."
The 20250508 module list now uses "Pointer-Masking Extensions" instead of "Pointer Masking" and uses extension-qualified names for Smctr, Svvptc, and Ssqosid.
The 20250508 change log entries now end the Ssqosid and Pointer-Masking bullets with periods; the first document omitted punctuation on those bullets.
Compatible changes since version 1.12 now state synchronization requirements for changing both PBMTE and ADUE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg; the first document mentioned only PBMTE fields.
Added compatible-change bullet: "Defined the format of the memory-mapped msip registers."
The mseccfg/mseccfgh change entry no longer describes the CSRs as optional; it now says "Defined mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs" instead of "Defined optional mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs".
The sentence describing the debug specification changed from "The separate debug specification proposal describes" to "The separate debug specification describes".
Added clarification that in CSR address bits csr[9:8], the pattern 10 represents hypervisor CSRs.
The cross-reference for virtual-instruction exceptions changed from a section-number reference to a named reference, with no change to the exception rule itself.
CSR listing introduction changed from "Table 4-Table 8" to "Table 4-Table 7" because the machine-level CSR listing was consolidated and new indirect CSR mapping tables were added separately.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added a Supervisor Indirect CSR group listing siselect (0x150), sireg (0x151), sireg2 (0x152), sireg3 (0x153), sireg4 (0x155), sireg5 (0x156), and sireg6 (0x157).

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added a Supervisor Timer Compare CSR group listing stimecmp (0x14D) and stimecmph (0x15D, RV32 only).

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Resource Management Configuration CSR srmcfg at 0x181.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Control Transfer Records Configuration CSRs sctrctl (0x14E), sctrstatus (0x14F), and sctrdepth (0x15F).

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Indirect CSR group listing vsiselect (0x250), vsireg (0x251), vsireg2 (0x252), vsireg3 (0x253), vsireg4 (0x255), vsireg5 (0x256), and vsireg6 (0x257).

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Timer Compare CSRs vstimecmp (0x24D) and vstimecmph (0x25D, RV32 only).

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Control Transfer Records Control Register vsctrctl at 0x24E.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Machine Indirect CSR group listing miselect (0x350), mireg (0x351), mireg2 (0x352), mireg3 (0x353), mireg4 (0x355), mireg5 (0x356), and mireg6 (0x357).

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added machine counter configuration CSRs mcyclecfg (0x321), minstretcfg (0x322), mcyclecfgh (0x721, RV32 only), and minstretcfgh (0x722, RV32 only).

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register mctrctl at 0x34E.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: Added a new CSR-listing subsection with indirect CSR mapping tables for M-mode, S-mode, and VS-mode select values, including AIA interrupt-priority and interrupt-enable/pending mappings, delegated counter mappings, and CTR entry mappings.
The statement "All bits that are reserved for future use must return zero when read" was moved from the Table 10 caption into standalone text before the Extensions field table.
Commentary changed "the repo around which development occurs" to "the project around which development occurs".
Requirement changed from "at least one hart must have a hart ID of zero" to "one hart must have a hart ID of zero".

differences.section: 3.1.6.2. Double Trap Control in mstatus Register
differences.description: Added that whether performance counters and timers are updated in the critical-error state is UNSPECIFIED.
MPRV description now applies to "explicit memory accesses" generally when MPRV=0, instead of saying only "loads and stores" behave normally.
TW field wording now says WFI may execute in "modes less privileged than M" and may raise in "modes less privileged than M" rather than "lower privilege modes" or "less-privileged modes".

U-mode WFI rule was clarified to state that when S-mode is implemented, executing WFI in U-mode causes an illegal-instruction exception regardless of TW unless it completes within a bounded time limit; the prior text did not explicitly say "regardless of the TW bit".
Removed the statement that a future revision might add a feature allowing S-mode to selectively permit WFI in U-mode when TW=0.

Machine software interrupt text no longer says memory-mapped control registers are used by remote harts or that a hart can write its own MSIP bit; it now says they are used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.

Added definition of a hart's memory-mapped msip register as a 32-bit read/write register with bits 31:1 reading zero and bit 0 containing MSIP, and added that changes to msip are eventually reflected in mip.MSIP.

Supervisor timer interrupt bits STIP/STIE now include Sstc behavior: if stimecmp is implemented, STIP is read-only and reflects the supervisor timer interrupt signal from stimecmp; otherwise STIP remains writable by M-mode software.
The second document contains a typographical change/defect: "reads ... return bitj 31-0" instead of "return bits 31-0".

Added Sstc-related TM-bit behavior: clearing mcounteren.TM blocks less-privileged access to stimecmp or vstimecmp, and setting it permits S-mode access to stimecmp/vstimecmp and VS-mode access to vstimecmp subject to hcounteren.TM.

mtval faulting-address rules now include hardware-error exceptions for instruction fetch, load, or store, whereas the first document listed breakpoint, address-misaligned, access-fault, and page-fault exceptions only.
Added rule that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, mtval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
Added commentary explaining that recording the instruction address in mtval for [C.]EBREAK breakpoint exceptions is redundant with mepc and is provided for backwards compatibility.
Rules for mtval on misaligned load/store faults and instruction faults now include hardware-error exceptions in addition to access-fault and page-fault exceptions.
Pointer-alignment formula was completed: it now states that if MXLEN is 8×n, then mconfigptr[log2 n - 1:0] must be zero; the first document had missing mathematical variables in this sentence.

Added requirement that after changing menvcfg.ADUE, executing SFENCE.VMA with rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 suffices to synchronize address-translation caches with respect to altered interpretation of PTE A/D bits, with hypervisor-related synchronization referenced separately.
STCE is now defined directly in menvcfg text as the Sstc STimecmp Enable bit, including behavior when STCE is zero; the first document only said the definition was furnished by the Sstc extension.

CBZE, CBCFE, and CBIE are now defined directly in menvcfg text, including Zicboz/Zicbom enable behavior, illegal-instruction behavior, and CBIE encodings; the first document only referenced Zicboz and Zicbom for these definitions.
PMM is now defined directly in menvcfg with a legal-values table for pointer masking and RV32 read-only-zero behavior; the first document only said PMM was furnished by the Smnpm extension.
mseccfg changed from an "optional 64-bit read/write register" to a register that exists if any extension adding an mseccfg field is implemented, and otherwise is reserved.
Added detailed Zkr definitions for SSEED and USEED fields controlling access to the seed CSR from modes less privileged than M.
Added Entropy Source Access Control table specifying seed CSR behavior in M, U, S/HS, and VS/VU modes for SSEED and USEED settings.

RLB, MMWP, and MML fields are now defined directly in mseccfg, including sticky behavior, locked-PMP modification rules, allowlist policy, and Machine Mode Lockdown rule changes; the first document only referenced Smepmp for these field definitions.
Added PMM field definition for Smmpm pointer masking in M-mode, including legal-values table, RV32 read-only-zero behavior, and an implementation note about supported address sizes versus XLEN-PMLEN.
mseccfgh existence rule now states it exists when XLEN=32 and mseccfg is implemented, and does not exist when XLEN=64; the first document only stated it exists for XLEN=32 and not XLEN=64.
Added that mseccfg.MML, mseccfg.MMWP, and mseccfg.RLB are reset to 0 unless the platform mandates a different reset value.
Added that mseccfg.USEED and mseccfg.SSEED must have defined reset values and must not be left undefined after reset.

Reset commentary changed to say "To avoid ambiguity, mcause reset values may alias mcause values following synchronous exceptions" instead of the prior wording "mcause reset values may alias ... There should be no ambiguity".
AMOArithmetic support is now specified as all RISC-V AMOs defined by the A extension, rather than all RISC-V AMOs without qualification.
Added Zacas AMOCASW, AMOCASD, and AMOCASQ PMA support levels and their relationship to AMOArithmetic.
Added that AMOCASW/D/Q require AMOArithmetic-level support because AMOCAS.W/D/Q need arithmetic comparison and swap capability.
Added that AMOs specified by the Zabha extension require the same level of support as the corresponding A-extension or Zacas instructions.
Scope of the misaligned atomicity granule PMA now explicitly includes compressed encodings of the listed AMOs, base loads/stores, and F/D/Q loads/stores.
PMP access-fault rules now explicitly cover cache-block management instructions as requiring read permission and cache-block zero instructions as requiring write permission.

Added commentary that address 2^(XLEN+2) cannot be represented as the top of a TOR range, with the example that an RV32 system with 34-bit physical addresses cannot use TOR PMP for the uppermost word of memory.
Removed the statement that if the current XLEN is greater than MXLEN, PMP address registers are zero-extended from MXLEN to XLEN bits for address matching.
The description of the L bit now says R/W/X permissions are "additionally enforced" on M-mode accesses, clarifying its effect in addition to entry locking.

PMP matching semantics were rewritten in terms of individual memory operations rather than an access as a whole; the lowest-numbered PMP entry matching any byte of a memory operation determines whether that operation succeeds.

Added an introductory rule that on some implementations misaligned loads, stores, and instruction fetches may be decomposed into multiple memory operations and PMP checking is performed independently on each operation.
The paragraph about partial visibility of misaligned stores was moved from the end of the section to the start and explicitly tied to RVWMO memory-operation decomposition.

RV32 state-enable CSR wording changed from saying the listed registers are 32-bit with corresponding high-half CSRs to saying there are CSR addresses for accessing the upper 32 bits of corresponding machine-level and hypervisor CSRs.
The introductory explanation no longer names the two mechanisms SMAP and SMEP; it now describes them generically as two mechanisms that prevent OS access to and execution of unprivileged memory.

The statement about existing RISC-V support was rephrased from support for SMAP through sstatus.SUM and SMEP through U-bit execution denial to support for the former through sstatus.SUM and the latter by denying supervisor execution of pages marked U.
The separate "6.1. Introduction" heading was removed; introductory material now appears directly under the Smepmp chapter heading.
Added a sentence stating that the rationale guiding development of Smepmp is included in Appendix A.
Threat-model text was revised to describe attacks mitigated through the virtual-memory system rather than through SMAP/SMEP terminology.

The first document's "6.2. Proposal" detailed definition of mseccfg, RLB, MMWP, MML, and their rules was removed from the Smepmp chapter and replaced by a short reference to the mseccfg section plus a summary truth table and visual representation.
Section heading changed from "Proposal" to "Smepmp Physical Memory Protection Rules".

The Smepmp rationale was moved out of Chapter 6 into new Appendix A.1 as historical rationale, with minor wording changes such as "proposal" becoming "extension" and explicit reference to the new mseccfg CSR.
Removed the sentence listing CSR numbers for mcyclecfg, minstretcfg, mcyclecfgh, and minstretcfgh from the Smcntrpmf chapter; the CSR numbers are now present in the consolidated CSR listing.
The implicit read of mnepc when IALIGN=32 was corrected from the MRET instruction to the MNRET instruction.
Chapter title changed from "Smcdeleg Counter Delegation Extension" to "Smcdeleg/Ssccfg Counter Delegation Extensions".
Introductory text changed from singular "This extension provides" and "This extension also defines" to plural "These extensions provide" and "These extensions also defines".
Added requirement that, for a RISC-V hardware platform, Smcdeleg and Ssccfg must always be implemented in tandem.

The note that hpmeventi/cyclecfg/instretcfg represent subsets of machine state was expanded into normative text specifying that if Sscofpmf or Smcntrpmf is implemented, bit 62 (MINH) is read-only zero when accessed through sireg*.
The rules describing illegal-instruction cases while siselect is in 0x40-0x5F were reformatted into inline asterisk-separated text in the parsed second document; content is otherwise retained.
Commentary now states that mvip, mvien, and hvien are defined by Smaia/Ssaia; the first document mentioned only mvien and hvien alongside hypervisor-defined hvip.
Added that for a not-taken branch, ctrtarget holds the PC of the next sequential instruction following the branch.
Added Control Transfer Records Management Instructions entry for SCTRCLR with encoding 0001000 00100 00000 000 00000 1110011.
The SRET cross-reference in the SPP description changed from the trap-return instruction section to a named "Other Privileged Instructions" reference.

STIP behavior now explicitly distinguishes systems without Sstc, where STIP is set and cleared by the execution environment, from systems with Sstc, where STIP reflects stimecmp and is set or cleared by writing stimecmp relative to time.
stval faulting-address rules now include hardware-error exceptions for instruction fetch, load, or store; the first document did not include hardware-error exceptions in these stval rules.
Added rule that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, stval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
Rules for stval on misaligned load/store faults and instruction faults now include hardware-error exceptions in addition to access-fault and page-fault exceptions.
Added detailed Zicboz CBZE definition for U-mode CBO.ZERO enablement through senvcfg and its dependence on S-mode enablement.
Added detailed Zicbom CBCFE definition for U-mode CBO.CLEAN and CBO.FLUSH enablement through senvcfg and its dependence on S-mode enablement.
Added detailed Zicbom CBIE definition for U-mode CBO.INVAL, including reserved encoding 10b and behavior for encodings 01b and 11b.
Added Ssnpm PMM field definition in senvcfg, including legal-values table for pointer masking in U/VU mode and RV32 read-only-zero behavior.

Added Supervisor Timer (stimecmp) Register section under Supervisor CSRs, incorporating the 64-bit stimecmp/stimecmph definition, STIP pending condition, interrupt timing behavior, and SBI compatibility commentary formerly located in the Sstc chapter.

Commentary about stale translations after PTE modification was expanded: the second document states that any older translation since the last subsuming SFENCE.VMA may be used, subsequent accesses may use different translations from that set, and behavior remains otherwise well-defined.

Added that the stale-translation property applies even when the virtual-address width for the cached translation differs from the width currently specified by satp.MODE, and that global translations may be used regardless of ASID and virtual-address width.

Added statement that no SFENCE.VMA is required after enabling or disabling pointer masking because pointer masking applies only to the effective address and does not affect memory-management data structures.
PTE permission fault rules now explicitly include cache-block management instructions as load-like for read permission and cache-block zero instructions as store-like for write permission.
A/D-bit update rule now says the PTE update must atomically perform all page-table walk checks, replacing the first document's "tablewalk checks" wording.
Step 9 now specifies that the PMA/PMP check is for a store to the PTE at address a+va.vpn[i]×PTESIZE, rather than a generic store to pte.
Step 9 now explicitly says that after setting pte.a and possibly pte.d, the updated pte is stored to the PTE address; the first document did not explicitly state the store in the atomic substeps.
Sv48 leaf page sizes are now explicitly listed as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, and 512 GiB terapages instead of the generic "pages, megapages, gigapages, and terapages" wording.

Sv57 leaf page sizes are now explicitly listed as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, 512 GiB terapages, and 256 TiB petapages instead of the generic "pages, megapages, gigapages, terapages, and petapages" wording.
Svnapot is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.
Added that if the hypervisor extension is also implemented, Svnapot is also supported in G-stage translation.
Svpbmt is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.
Svinval is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.

The exception-permission paragraph was split so that the U-mode illegal-instruction rule for SINVAL.VMA/HINVAL.VVMA/HINVAL.GVMA is a separate sentence from the mstatus.TVM rule; behavior is otherwise unchanged.
Svadu is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.
Svvptc is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.
Added new Svrsw60t59b extension specifying that bits 60-59 of PTEs are reserved for supervisor software and ignored by the implementation.
Added that when the Hypervisor extension is implemented, bits 60-59 of G-stage PTEs are also reserved for supervisor software and ignored by the implementation.
Added dependency statement that Svrsw60t59b depends on Sv39.

Added commentary explaining that operating systems use reserved PTE bits for metadata and that without this extension Sv39/Sv39x4 require page-fault or guest-page-fault exceptions when bits 60-59 are nonzero.
Ssqosid is now a subsection of the Supervisor-Level ISA chapter instead of a separate top-level chapter.

The Sstc chapter was shortened to an overview and now references the stimecmp, vstimecmp, menvcfg, and henvcfg sections for normative details; the detailed subsections for machine/supervisor additions, hypervisor additions, and environment config support were removed from the Sstc chapter.
Removed separate Sstc subsections 19.1, 19.1.1, 19.1.2, 19.1.3, 19.1.4, 19.2, 19.2.1, 19.2.2, 19.2.3, and 19.3; their content was integrated into the relevant CSR sections.
Added HUPMM field definition for Ssnpm pointer masking behavior of HLV.* and HSV.* instructions in U-mode when accesses are performed as VU-mode.
Added commentary that the hypervisor should copy the guest's senvcfg.PMM value to hstatus.HUPMM before invoking HLV.* or HSV.* instructions in U-mode.

differences.section: 15.2.3. Hypervisor Interrupt (hvip, hip, and hie) Registers
differences.description: VSTIP behavior now includes Sstc: hip.VSTIP is the OR of hvip.VSTIP and, when Sstc is implemented, the timer interrupt signal from vstimecmp, and specifies how writes to vstimecmp set or clear it.
Added detailed Sstc STCE definition in henvcfg, including VS-mode vstimecmp enablement and virtual-instruction exception behavior when STCE is zero.
Added detailed Zicboz CBZE definition in henvcfg for VS/VU CBO.ZERO execution, HS-qualified checks, and virtual-instruction exception behavior.

Added detailed Zicbom CBCFE and CBIE definitions in henvcfg for VS/VU cache-block clean, flush, and invalidate instructions, including HS-qualified checks, CBIE encodings, and flush-vs-invalidate behavior.
Added Ssnpm PMM field definition in henvcfg, including legal-values table for VS-mode pointer masking and RV32 read-only-zero behavior.

Added Sstc behavior for hcounteren.TM: clearing it blocks VS-mode access to vstimecmp via stimecmp with a virtual-instruction exception if mcounteren.TM is set, and setting both bits permits VS-mode access.

differences.section: 15.2.18. Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (vsatp) Register
differences.description: Commentary changed from saying misspeculatively executed HLV/HLVX/HSV instructions must not set VS-stage A bits to saying mispredicted HLV/HLVX/HSV instructions must not set VS-stage A bits.

Added Virtual Supervisor Timer (vstimecmp) Register section under Hypervisor and VS CSRs, incorporating the 64-bit vstimecmp/vstimecmph definition and VSTIP pending condition formerly in the Sstc chapter.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: Added that memory accesses performed by HLVX.* instructions are not subject to pointer masking.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: Added commentary explaining HLVX.* pointer-masking exemption because HLVX.* emulates implicit instruction-fetch access from guest memory and pointer masking also does not apply when MXR is set.
The G bit in G-stage PTEs is now described as "currently not used" rather than "reserved for future standard use"; software-clear and hardware-ignore requirements remain.
Synchronization rules now include ADUE changes as well as PBMTE changes for menvcfg and henvcfg, and state that fences synchronize altered interpretation of PBMT and A/D bit fields respectively.
World-switch commentary now says henvcfg.PBMTE/ADUE should be switched after zeroing vsatp and before writing the new vsatp value; the first document mentioned only henvcfg.PBMTE.

Added hypervisor section describing interaction of pointer masking with two-stage address translation, including GPA width, vsatp.MODE=Bare, hgatp.MODE, senvcfg.PMM/henvcfg.PMM, and required HFENCE.GVMA cases.
In the hypervisor trap cause table, interrupt exception code 13 changed from "Reserved for counter-overflow interrupt" to "Counter-overflow interrupt".

differences.section: 15.6.3. Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for mtinst or htinst
differences.description: Figure captions for transformed load and store instructions were shortened by removing "noncompressed" from the caption wording, while the surrounding text still says the instructions are not compressed.
The HWASAN applicability parenthetical changed from "U, S and M" to "U, S, and M".
The definition of effective address now says implicit accesses include "page-table walks" instead of "page table walks".

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Compressed instruction list no longer includes C.LQSP and C.SQSP as explicit memory accesses subject to pointer masking.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: The Hypervisor Extension bullet now refers to the hstatus section for HLV/HSV pointer-masking cases instead of the old Pointer Masking ISA extension subsection.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Hardware CSR write and debug trigger matching rules now qualify pointer masking with "when applicable"; the first document stated pointer masking is applied without that qualifier.
Definitions of Ssnpm, Smnpm, and Smmpm now point to the relevant CSR sections where PMM/HUPMM behavior is defined instead of saying details are in a separate ISA Extensions section.

Removed the separate "ISA Extensions" chapter subsection that directly defined the PMM WARL field and its table for all pointer-masking extensions; equivalent PMM definitions were moved into menvcfg, senvcfg, henvcfg, hstatus, and mseccfg sections.

Removed separate subsections Ssnpm, Smnpm, Smmpm, Interaction with SFENCE.VMA, and Interaction with Two-Stage Address Translation from the Pointer Masking chapter; their content was redistributed to supervisor, hypervisor, and machine CSR/fence sections.
The Number of Masked Bits section is now part of Background as Section 18.2.8 rather than part of an ISA Extensions section as Section 24.3.6.
The separate top-level "24.3. ISA Extensions" heading and its introductory statement that Smmpm, Smnpm, and Ssnpm do not add any new CSRs were removed.

differences.section: Appendix A: Historical Rationale for Extensions
differences.description: Added a new Appendix A explaining that historical rationale for ISA extensions will be collected chronologically and added over time.
Added Appendix A.1 containing the historical rationale for Smepmp, largely corresponding to the first document's former Section 6.4 Rationale.
Bibliography is no longer a standalone final section; in the second document the bibliography entries appear appended at the end of Appendix A.1 after malformed text "== Bibliography".
